128.reason: [After skipping to the newline, decrementing index by one causes the newline character to be processed again in the next iteration, potentially leading to incorrect line number tracking and tokenization.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After the while loop, index points to '\n', then index-- makes it point to the character before '\n', but the outer loop will increment index again, causing the newline to be skipped without incrementing lineNum. This can lead to incorrect line number tracking.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [After skipping to the newline, decrementing index by one causes the newline character to be processed again in the next iteration, potentially leading to incorrect line number tracking and tokenization.]

137.reason: [After the while loop, index points to '/', then index++ makes it point to the character after '/', but the outer loop will increment index again, causing the character after the comment to be skipped. This can lead to missing tokens.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After the while loop, index points to '/', then index++ makes it point to the character after '/', but the outer loop will increment index again, causing the character after the comment to be skipped. This can lead to missing tokens.]

237.reason: [If the character is not followed by a closing quote (e.g., invalid char constant), no error is recorded and the loop continues without proper handling, potentially causing incorrect tokenization or missing error detection.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the character is not followed by a closing quote (e.g., invalid char constant), no error is recorded and the loop continues without proper handling, potentially causing incorrect tokenization or missing error detection.]

239.reason: [Missing error handling when char const is malformed; also fails to advance index properly in all cases, and doesn't handle single quote with no closing quote or invalid length]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [If the character constant is malformed (e.g., missing closing quote), the code does not handle the error and does not increment index properly, which can cause an infinite loop or incorrect tokenization.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing error handling when char const is malformed; also fails to advance index properly in all cases, and doesn't handle single quote with no closing quote or invalid length]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This code does not handle invalid or unterminated character constants. If the next character after '\'' is not followed by a closing '\'', no error is reported, and the lexer silently skips it, which violates the requirement to report lexical errors. Additionally, it fails to advance `index` properly in the malformed case, potentially causing infinite loops or missed errors.]

233.reason: [Potential index out of bounds: does not check if index+2 is within input length]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Potential index out of bounds: does not check if index+2 is within input length]

154.reason: [hasNext(input) only checks index < input.length(), so when index points to the last character, input.charAt(index + 1) throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [hasNext(input) only checks index < input.length(), so when index points to the last character, input.charAt(index + 1) throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The hasNext method only checks if index < input.length(). When index points to the last character of the string, hasNext returns true, but input.charAt(index + 1) attempts to access an out-of-bounds index, causing a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.]

162.reason: [hasNext(input) only checks index < input.length(), so when index points to the last character, input.charAt(index + 1) throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [hasNext(input) only checks index < input.length(), so when index points to the last character, input.charAt(index + 1) throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: accessing input.charAt(index + 1) without verifying that index + 1 is strictly less than input.length() will throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when the character appears at the very end of the input.]
